steps.title: Use the sinuosity threshold
steps.content: Sinuosity is the ratio of stream length to valley length. The notes say ratios greater than 1.5:1 are high-sinuosity, while ratios less than or equal to 1.5:1 are low-sinuosity. Fort Nelson is high-sinuosity, and Wadi Al-Hamd is low-sinuosity.

steps.title: Make a direct comparison
steps.content: Choice A compares locations, not sinuosity. Choice B states the same calculation method for both rivers, and Choice D only says how to calculate sinuosity. The needed sentence compares the ratios and shows Fort Nelson's exceeds Wadi Al-Hamd's. The answer is C.
